The meat is super tender, which I love. I'm not a fan of fatty meat, so this is perfect. Of course,
it's best with a piece of fresh kimchi. I like these Korean green peppers, but I'm a little nervous
because this one is small, which means it's probably quite hot.
My mouth is on fire. That was a slow burn. At first, I thought it was okay, but that heat creeps up

I hope I make it there alive. This bus is a little crazy. It's like a roller coaster.
I love Gamcheon Culture Village. And some people even call it the centaurini of Korea because of
it. Colorful hillside houses and the beautiful view of the ocean. I heard that this neighborhood used
to be a tiny mountainside village, but they transformed it into an art town with sculptures,
murals, and super cute cafes. Two iced Americanos, please. To go. Yep. I'm grabbing a coffee at this
cute cafe that I found on TikTok. They deliver it to you in a mini hot air balloon. You want to try?
That was the cutest thing ever. And they're so friendly here. If you're in Gamcheon Culture Village,
come to Blue House on the stairs. Korean cafes are next level. How do they come up with this stuff?

but I already got my souvenir. Gamcheon is so colorful and charming, but it's time to move on to my next
destination. Jaregachi Market is one of the most famous seafood markets in Korea. It's the
perfect place for seafood lovers. And since Busan is a coastal city, everything is especially
fresh here. I can't believe the size of some of the fish here. They're huge. It's so lively here.
There are tons of vendors. I love the energy. Today, I just took a look around the market,
but I'm definitely coming back to enjoy some of the delicious seafood next time. Right now,

Never. This area is always super packed, and it kind of reminds me of Myeongdong because there are so

Sunset time. The best time of day. I really wanted to ride in the capsules, but the tickets were

instead. It runs along the same route, and they take walk-ups. We'll be departing shortly. Yellow,
my favorite color. Look at that. Yellow, number one.
The train ride was only about 10 minutes long, and maybe this is an obvious piece of advice,
but make sure you come in the daytime. We couldn't see anything, but in my defense,
we came at the perfect time of day, but there was no availability, and we had to wait, and it got dark.
But it isn't a true travel day without some mishaps. That's what makes traveling so memorable.
I'm hungry, so now I'm heading to a restaurant to eat some dinner.
It's time to eat Busan's signature noodle dish, Mirmyeon, and I came to one of the OG spots to try it.
Gaya Mirmyeon. Do you like bibim or muneung more? Personally, I like a combination of the two.
Bibim muneung is the best. Yes, exactly. You're catching on. Of course, I need to add just a touch of
mustard and vinegar for that extra flavor. So refreshing. I love this. Cold noodles are one of my
personal favorite Korean foods. I love all cold noodles. This one tastes like jjormyeon.
I love jjormyeon. Manjoo is good too. I'm not going to eat it. I'm not going to eat it.
Clear it, but don't be fooled. I had some help from the cameraman. Lots of help.
This time, I definitely preferred the bibim muneung. It was awesome.
After a long day of sightseeing, I'm wrapping up my Busan adventure here at Guanali Beach.
You can grab a drink at a beachfront cafe, sit back, relax, and just enjoy the beautiful night view.
There are also lots of street performances here, which are always fun to watch.
Personally, I just love walking on the beach and taking it all in. Today was like a mini staycation
for me. It was so much fun. Check out the pins, comment below, and I will see you next time. Goodbye!
